questions.question: List three characteristics of time-series data and explain why it is so prevalent in IoT.
expectedPoints: Data points are indexed or recorded in chronological order.
Often generated at regular intervals by sensors.
Used for tracking changes over time, forecasting, and anomaly detection.
Prevalent because most sensors (temp, humidity, pressure) take repeated measurements over time, creating natural time-series datasets.

questions.question: Compare JSON and Protocol Buffers (Protobuf) for IoT data transmission. Discuss two advantages and one disadvantage of each.
expectedPoints: JSON Advantages: Human-readable, which aids debugging; Highly flexible and schema-less, easy to modify data structure; Universally supported across programming languages and platforms.
JSON Disadvantage: Verbose text format leads to larger payload size, consuming more bandwidth.
Protobuf Advantages: Extremely compact binary format, minimizing bandwidth usage; Very fast serialization/deserialization speed; Enforces a strict schema, reducing data errors.
Protobuf Disadvantage: Not human-readable, requires special tools to decode; Requires upfront schema definition (.proto files) and less flexible to change.
